title: [Feature]: Evaluate legacy and specialized database sinks
problem: Oracle GoldenGate connectivity material lists legacy and specialized systems such as Sybase Adaptive Server Enterprise, HPE Enscribe, HPE NonStop SQL/MP, HPE NonStop SQL/MX, and FairCom DB. nats-sinks does not yet define whether these systems are in scope.
proposal: Research whether legacy and specialized database targets should become documented out-of-scope items, generic JDBC candidates, or dedicated sink feature requests. The evaluation should consider driver availability, licensing, transaction semantics, operator demand, security review effort, and testability.
users: Enterprise operators with legacy transaction systems and maintainers deciding how far nats-sinks should extend beyond modern cloud and database connectors.
delivery_semantics: No ACK-gated sink should be accepted until a target-specific commit boundary and idempotent duplicate strategy are documented.
security: Legacy protocols and drivers must be treated as high-risk. Credentials, endpoints, table names, and payloads must be redacted, and unsafe dynamic driver loading must be rejected.
acceptance: Research classifies each legacy target as dedicated sink, generic profile, or not planned.
Driver and security risks are documented.
Any accepted target receives a separate feature request.
No implementation starts from this grouped issue.
tests: Research-only initially. Future implementations require fake drivers, injection tests, and gated live tests.
documentation: Add legacy database sink evaluation notes to roadmap documentation.
closeout: Close after research release and creation of follow-up issues if needed.
